adams_theorem.statement: The only Hopf fibrations S^(2n-1) -> S^n are for n=2,4,8
adams_theorem.equivalently: R(1), C(2), H(4), O(8) are the only normed division algebras
adams_theorem.hurwitz_1898: Only dimensions 1, 2, 4, 8 admit normed division algebras
adams_theorem.adams_1960: Only S^1, S^3, S^7 are H-spaces (admit continuous multiplication)

why_not_quaternionic_qm.tensor_product_fails: H is non-commutative, so H-bilinear maps are problematic
why_not_quaternionic_qm.no_spectral_theorem: Self-adjoint operators over H don't decompose cleanly
why_not_quaternionic_qm.complex_suffices: C + SU(2) symmetry recovers all quaternionic structure needed
why_not_quaternionic_qm.su2_is_already_H: The gate group SU(2) = unit quaternions, so H is already present

why_not_octonionic_qm.non_associative: O is non-associative, breaking operator composition: (AB)C != A(BC)
why_not_octonionic_qm.no_matrix_algebra: Mat_n(O) is not an algebra for n >= 2
why_not_octonionic_qm.exceptional_appearances: O appears in E8, Jordan algebras (J3(O)), but not as QIT base
why_not_octonionic_qm.hopf_O_exists: S^7 -> S^15 -> S^8 exists but has no standard QM interpretation

division_algebra_tower_for_qit.C_dim2: Standard QIT -- Hilbert space base field
division_algebra_tower_for_qit.H_dim4: SU(2) = unit quaternions = qubit gate group = S^3
division_algebra_tower_for_qit.O_dim8: Exceptional structures only, not QIT base field
division_algebra_tower_for_qit.answer: d=2 QIT uses C, but SU(2) = unit quaternion sphere S^3 is the gate group
